rxn_class_summary: Visible-light energy-transfer-catalyzed dearomative [2π+2σ] cycloaddition of thiophenes with bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes (BCBs) and dearomative [2π+2π] cycloaddition of oxazoles/thiazoles with electron-deficient alkenes

key_intermediates.name: triplet excited state of 2-benzoylthiophene (1a*) with spin density highest at C5 (0.69) and C2 (0.51)
triplet 1,4-diradical I from C5 addition of 1a* to BCB 2a (cyclobutyl radical + thiophene-centered radical)
ring-opened diradical II after thiophene C-S/C-C cleavage at C4
singlet diene intermediate III formed after ISC and radical-radical recombination
tricyclic product 3a from disrotatory photochemical 4π-electrocyclization of III
triplet excited state of 2-phenyloxazole (5a*) with spin density at C5 (0.61) and C4 (0.34)
triplet 1,4-diradical IV from C5 addition of 5a* to acrylonitrile
2-oxa-4-azabicyclo[3.2.0]hept-3-ene cycloadduct 6a after ISC and radical-radical recombination

electron_flow: No net electron transfer: photoexcited Ir photocatalyst (*fac-Ir(dFppy)3 or Ir-F) transfers triplet energy to the azole/thiophene substrate, generating a triplet biradical that adds across the alkene/BCB; the resulting triplet 1,4-diradical undergoes ISC and radical-radical recombination (with optional ring opening and 4π-electrocyclization for thiophenes) to deliver the cycloadduct.

probe_reagents.name: thioxanthone (low triplet-energy photocatalyst control)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation with TXT for thiophene substrate, consistent with required ET > 60 kcal/mol; only partial activity (39%) in oxazole series

probe_reagents.name: 4-fluorophenyl-substituted thiophene 1ab
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction stops at the diene stage giving 3ab, confirming intermediate III

probe_reagents.name: quantum yield measurement
probe_reagents.observation: Φ = 0.13 (thiophene) and Φ = 0.09 (oxazole), ruling out radical chain mechanism

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 1b, 5a, 7a vs Ir photocatalyst potentials
probe_reagents.observation: Substrate oxidation (~1.9 V vs SCE) and reduction (~ -2.36 V vs SCE) are not thermodynamically accessible to excited Ir-F (PC*/PC- = +1.21, PC+/PC* = -0.89 V), ruling out photoredox/SET pathway

probe_reagents.name: Stern-Volmer luminescence quenching
probe_reagents.observation: Thiophene 1a (Ksv ≈ 150) and oxazole 5a (Ksv ≈ 13.9) quench *Ir; BCB 2a and acrylonitrile 4b do not, supporting substrate activation via energy transfer

probe_reagents.name: UV-vis absorption
probe_reagents.observation: Only the Ir photocatalyst absorbs at 405/450 nm; direct excitation of substrate gives no product, ruling out direct photoexcitation

probe_reagents.name: Mulliken spin-population (DFT) analysis on 1a* and 5a*
probe_reagents.observation: Highest spin density at C5 (0.69 in 1a*, 0.61 in 5a*) rationalizes regioselectivity of radical addition

notes: Photocatalyst triplet energy threshold (>60 kcal/mol) correlates with yield; thioxanthone (ET = 65.5) inactive for thiophenes but partially active for oxazoles. For thiophenes, the diradical I undergoes thiophene ring cleavage (C4-radical drives C-S cleavage) to diene III, then disrotatory 4π-electrocyclization gives the 2-thiabicyclo[3.2.0] product; for oxazoles, no ring-opening, direct biradical closure gives [2π+2π] cycloadduct. An unusual product 3j from 4-phenyl-2-acetothiophene is proposed to form via photochemically allowed suprafacial 1,3-sigmatropic shift or stepwise 1,3-migration of the styrenic cyclobutene. Esters and Weinreb amides on thiophene are unreactive (insufficient ET lowering).
